events.title: Angel Investor Ping
events.prompt: An angel investor saw your pitch deck and wants to meet. They'd invest $50k for 15% equity. Your runway is getting short.

choices.label: Take the deal — runway matters more right now
Negotiate for 10% equity instead
Decline — bootstrap a bit longer

events.explanation: Equity is permanent — once sold, it's gone. Negotiate hard, and only raise when you truly need the runway.

events.title: Server Meltdown
events.prompt: Your app went viral overnight and the servers crashed. You need to scale up immediately or lose new users.

choices.label: Emergency scale-up — pay for premium cloud tier
Put up a waitlist page and scale gradually

events.explanation: Scaling costs money, but losing early users during traction is worse. A waitlist preserves hype while buying you time.

events.title: Co-founder Conversation
events.prompt: Your co-founder wants to renegotiate the equity split from 50-50 to 60-40 in their favor because they handle all the tech.

choices.label: Agree — keeping the partnership healthy matters
Propose vesting schedules tied to milestones instead
Refuse — the split was agreed on

events.explanation: Vesting schedules protect both founders. They tie equity to contribution over time, preventing disputes if someone leaves early.

events.title: First Paying Customer!
events.prompt: Someone just paid for your product! It's only $29/month, but it's real revenue. How do you reinvest?

choices.label: Reinvest into marketing to get more customers
Save it — build a cash reserve first
Use it to improve the product

events.explanation: Early revenue is a signal, not a salary. Reinvesting wisely — whether in growth, product, or reserves — compounds over time.

events.title: Growing Pains
events.prompt: You can't handle all the work alone anymore. You can hire a part-time employee or outsource to a freelancer.

choices.label: Hire part-time — invest in the team
Outsource the project to a freelancer
Grind through it yourself for now

events.explanation: Full-time hires cost more but build culture. Freelancers are flexible but may not stay. Grinding alone risks burnout — the hidden cost.

events.title: Market Signal: Pivot?
events.prompt: User data shows people love one feature but ignore the rest of your product. Should you pivot to focus on just that feature?

choices.label: Pivot — double down on what works
Stay the course — the full vision matters
A/B test for two more weeks

events.explanation: Data-driven pivots are a startup superpower. Many successful companies started as something completely different.

events.title: Tax Season Reality
events.prompt: It's tax time and you owe $2,000 in self-employment tax. You didn't set money aside for this.

choices.label: Pay it from savings — lesson learned
Set up a payment plan with the tax authority
Hire an accountant to find deductions

events.explanation: Self-employment means paying your own taxes quarterly. Set aside 25-30% of income for taxes to avoid year-end surprises.

events.title: Pitch Competition Invite
events.prompt: You're invited to a startup pitch competition. First prize is $10k, but prep takes a full week away from building product.

choices.label: Enter the competition — exposure + prize money
Skip it and keep building

events.explanation: Pitch competitions build your network and storytelling skills. But time spent not building product has a real opportunity cost.
